vanillekipferl.name: Vanillekipferl
instructions: Stir together all-purpose flour, almond flour, and 1/2 cup plus 2 tablespoons sugar in a large bowl. Add butter and egg yolks; knead by hand until a smooth dough forms.
Divide dough into quarters and form each piece into a rectangle. Wrap each rectangle in plastic wrap. Chill dough in the refrigerator until firm, at least 1 hour, and up to overnight.

Make vanilla sugar: Place 1/2 cup plus 1 tablespoon white sugar in a food processor. Cut each vanilla bean in half lengthwise and scrape seeds from each half with the back of the knife; add to sugar. Pulse until seeds are evenly dispersed. Pour vanilla sugar into a shallow bowl and set aside.
Preheat the oven to 177°C (175 degrees C). Line 2 large baking sheets with parchment paper.

Cut 1 portion of dough into 6 equal pieces, keeping remaining dough chilled. Roll each piece evenly into a 1/2-inch-thick log on a lightly floured surface. Cut each log crosswise into 2-inch pieces and gently roll or pinch ends of each piece into points. Arrange cookies on the prepared baking sheets.
Bake in the preheated oven until bottoms are lightly golden but tops are still pale, 10 to 12 minutes. Remove from the oven and let stand for 2 minutes.
Roll warm cookies in vanilla sugar, brushing off excess sugar. Transfer cookies to a wire rack to cool completely. Repeat with remaining dough, baking in batches.

vegan-carrot-cake-recipe-comedera.name: Vegan Carrot Cake Recipe
vegan-carrot-cake-recipe-comedera.original_name: Receta de bizcocho vegano de zanahoria

vegan-meatballs-argiro.name: Vegan Meatballs
instructions: We'll preferably use boiled black beans. Alternatively, you can choose any legume you prefer and like.
After boiling, drain the beans very well so that they do not leave moisture in the mixture.
Place all the ingredients in the blender and grind until a uniform paste is formed.
With a spatula, clean the walls of the blender and grind again, to make sure that all the materials are properly pulped and bonded together.
The final mixture will resemble dense, sticky dough.
We take small amounts with a spoon and make the meatballs. At this stage we can, if we want, freeze the mixture.
Fry the meatballs in hot oil or, alternatively, bake them in the oven.

For baking in the oven, spread them with olive oil and place them on a baking pan line with non-stick baking paper. Bake in a well preheated ocean at 200°C, in the air, in the medium grill, for about 20 minutes.
Vegan meatballs are eaten straight or accompanied ideally with tomato sauce and pasta.

vegetable-fajitas-recipe-comedera.name: Vegetable Fajitas Recipe

ingredients.item: 3  pimentones (rojo, verde y amarillo) (cortados en julianas)

instructions: Vamos a iniciar nuestra receta organizando los ingredientes.
A frying pan with olive oil should be heated. Once hot, add onion with garlic and spray till clear.
Es turno de agregar la zanahoria y los pimentones, mezcla y cocina por 5 minutos. Mezcla ocasionalmente para que los vegetales se doren por todos lados.

vegetarian-pho-vietnamese-noodle-soup.name: Vegetarian Pho (vietnamese Noodle Soup)
instructions: Place vegetable stock, onion, soy sauce, garlic, cinnamon sticks, ground ginger, star anise, and bay leaves in a large pot; bring to a boil. Reduce heat, cover, and simmer until flavors combine, 30 to 45 minutes. Remove solids with a slotted spoon and keep broth hot.
Place noodles in a large bowl and cover with boiling water. Set aside until noodles are softened, 8 to 10 minutes. Drain and rinse thoroughly. Divide noodles among 6 serving bowls.
Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat until shimmering. Add tofu in a single layer and fry, in batches, until golden brown, about 6 minutes per side.
Simmer fried tofu and mushrooms in broth until heated through, about 5 minutes. Transfer to serving bowls. Top with scallions and cilantro. Ladle in hot broth.
Serve lime wedges, jalapeno peppers, bean sprouts, and basil alongside for garnishing each bowl.

veggie-fried-rice.name: Veggie Fried Rice
instructions: In a wok or deep skillet, heat the oil over high heat until shimmering. Add the carrot, onion, and garlic and cook until the onion is translucent.
Add the bell peppers and broccoli and cook for 3-4 minutes, until tender. Push the cooked vegetables to the sides of the pan.
Pour the beaten eggs into the empty part of the pan. Stir until scrambled, then mix in with the vegetables.
Add the peas, corn, rice, soy sauce, sesame oil, and black pepper. Mix well, then let the rice cook until slightly crispy.
Enjoy!

veggie-packed-chow-mein.name: Veggie-packed Chow Mein

ingredients.prep: par cooked according to package instructions

instructions: In a wok or deep skillet over medium-high heat, heat 2 tablespoons of cooking oil, then add the par-cooked noodles. Cook for 1-2 minutes, allowing the noodles to crisp, then flip and cook for another 1-2 minutes. Transfer to a plate and set aside.

In the same wok, heat the remaining tablespoon of cooking oil over medium-high heat and add the onion, bell pepper, broccoli, and carrot. Stir and cook for 2 minutes, then add the garlic and season with salt and pepper. Stir and continue to cook for 2 minutes, or until the onions are translucent.
Add the cabbage, shiitake mushrooms, snow peas, and corn.
In a small bowl, combine the water and cornstarch. Mix with a fork to dissolve the cornstarch, then add the soy sauce, hoisin sauce, rice wine, sugar, and white pepper. Mix well.
Pour the sauce over the vegetables. Mix well and cook for 3-5 minutes, or until the vegetables have softened.
Add the crispy noodles to the wok, mix well and add bean sprouts. Toss gently and cook for another 2-3 minutes, or until everything is well-combined. Garnish with sliced scallions.
Enjoy!

vodka-gochujang-pasta.name: Vodka Gochujang Pasta

ingredients.item: Passata (tomato purée) or finely chopped canned tomatoes
Light cream (18% fat) or oat-based creamer

instructions: Put 2 Tbsp. canola oil into a large frying pan on a medium to high heat, add 1 red onion, halved, thinly sliced, and 1 tsp. salt and cook, stirring, for 8 minutes, until onion is soft enough to cut easily with a wooden spoon.

Add 2 garlic cloves, crushed, fry 2 minutes, then add 1¾ cups passata (tomato purée) or finely chopped canned tomatoes, 1 Tbsp. gochujang, 1 Tbsp. tomato paste, and 3 Tbsp. vodka and leave it all to bubble away for 10 minutes, until the mix is quite paste-like. Stir in ¾ cup light cream (18% fat) or oat-based creamer and remaining 3 Tbsp. vodka, simmer a couple minutes, then take off the heat.
Cook 14 oz. rigatoni a minute less than the package instructions. Carefully scoop a mug (around 1 cup) of the cooking water out of the pasta pan, put this to one side, then drain the pasta.

Pop sauce back on the heat, stir in pasta and all the reserved pasta cooking water, then let it bubble away for a couple minutes, until sauce is nice and thick and clinging to pasta. Spoon into bowls and add a little salt and freshly ground black pepper if you wish.
